summary.brief: Morrisroe v. Pantano. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: In 1999, Viola was diagnosed with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and emphysema by pulmonologist Dr. Edward Diamond
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On September 29, 2009, Viola was admitted to the emergency room for stomach pain and shortness of breath.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On October 1, 2009, Dr. Pantano performed the bronchoscopy with Viola under conscious sedation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Viola’s lung began to rebleed. As a result, she developed progressive respiratory failure and cardiac arrest, which lead to her death.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Grodzin testified that Dr. Pantano deviated from the standard of care by proceeding with the bronchoscopy and performing the biopsies.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Grodzin

evidence_index.text: Dr. Grodzin opined that the bronchoscopy was not warranted because the September scan demonstrated that the mass was 'relatively stable, if not slightly smaller.'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Grodzin

evidence_index.text: Dr. Grodzin opined that 'a reasonable and careful physician would have informed [Viola] of the risks of the procedure including the risk of dying,'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Grodzin

evidence_index.text: Dr. Pantano testified that he discussed performing a bronchoscopy with Viola.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The judgment of the circuit court of Cook County is affirmed.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff’s counsel’s statements to the jury implied that the proper legal standard was what Viola would have done had she been informed of the risk of death and, therefore, were misleading.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Former smoker, COPD and emphysema history

initial_state.chief_complaint: Stomach pain and shortness of breath
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient with a history of COPD and emphysema presented with stomach pain and shortness of breath. Previous CT scans indicated a mass in the upper right lobe of the lung.
initial_state.initial_working_problem: Mass in the lung with differential including possible cancer
critical_findings: Mass in the upper right lobe of the lung

reveal.result_summary: Mass in the upper right lobe, size increase noted

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with acute symptoms

key_events.event: Patient admitted for stomach pain and shortness of breath

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with a lung mass and declining respiratory function

options.clinical_reasoning: Mass appeared to increase in size, potential malignancy
Mass was stable, bronchoscopy not warranted

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed mass had increased in size

expected_action_court.description: Bronchoscopy should not have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated mass was stable

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Mass was stable, bronchoscopy posed unnecessary risk
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed respiratory failure and cardiac arrest, leading to death

key_findings: Court found no breach of standard of care
Bronchoscopy was deemed reasonable given clinical context

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Monitoring could have avoided procedure risks

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to ED with stomach pain and shortness of breath

tests_performed.result: Mass in upper right lobe, size increase noted

allegations.allegation: Failure to provide proper informed consent

court_findings.finding: No breach of standard of care
court_findings.legal_significance: Bronchoscopy was reasonable given clinical context
